algorithm: New algorithm: This algorithm selects the next node with the highest ratio of the total sum of distances to all truck nodes to the distance between the current node and the next node, while also ensuring that at least one truck can reach that node.

Our algorithm, named 'SmartNextNode', selects the next node based on the node that is closest to the current node while taking into account the unvisited nodes, destination nodes, and also considers the potential waiting time at certain nodes to optimize truck routes.

Description: This algorithm selects the next node by finding the unvisited node with the shortest total distance (distance to current node + distance to destination node) and checks if it is reachable by any of the trucks.
code:
import numpy as np

def select_next_node(current_node, truck_nodes, destination_node, unvisited_nodes, distance_matrix):
    min_total_distance = np.inf
    next_node = None
    
    for node in unvisited_nodes:
        total_distance = distance_matrix[current_node][node] + distance_matrix[node][destination_node]
        is_reachable = any([distance_matrix[truck_node][node] < np.inf for truck_node in truck_nodes])
        if total_distance < min_total_distance and is_reachable:
            min_total_distance = total_distance
            next_node = node
    
    return next_node

algorithm: This algorithm selects the next node by finding the unvisited node with the shortest total distance to all truck nodes, while also ensuring that at least one truck can reach that node.

The new algorithm, named 'EfficientNextNode', selects the next node by considering the node that minimizes the total distance traveled by all trucks while ensuring that each truck has a fair distribution of nodes to visit.
